content:
SUBJECT: AI Gym Day 80: The claim audit

The free 14 days got the reps moving. The paid track is where the habit has to become professionally useful. Today belongs to judgement and risk: one practical workout, one usable output, one more piece of your AI working system.

TODAY'S WORKOUT
---------------
RESEARCH & DISCOVERY | PUSH | 22 min

THE BRIEF:
Audit claims before they become confident nonsense.

THE SITUATION:
Bring an AI-assisted draft with factual claims.

PROMPT SCAFFOLD:
Do not copy this blindly. Adapt it to your work.

"I am working on [real situation]. My goal is [specific outcome]. The audience or user is [person/team/customer/reader]. Here is the source material or context I am allowed to share: [paste safe material]. The constraints are [length, tone, deadline, format, privacy, things to avoid]. Help me with: Audit claims before they become confident nonsense. Before producing the final output, tell me what assumptions you are making and what information would improve the result."

YOUR REPS:
1. Ask the model to list every factual claim.

2. Mark each as source-backed, needs checking, opinion, or inference.

3. Remove, source, or soften every unchecked claim.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
Your output is good if:
- Every factual claim is classified.
- Unchecked claims are not left pretending.
- The final draft is more honest.

TRANSFER:
This transfers to privacy, bias checks, claim audits, escalation rules, red-team reviews, and any work where being wrong confidently would be more expensive than being slower carefully.

COACHING NOTE:
Fluent writing makes claims feel true. Claim audits interrupt that little spell.

BEHAVIOURAL DESIGN:
Use claim audit before publishing anything public. This is your implementation intention for today: after you finish the workout, decide exactly where this output will live and when you will use it again.

MINIMUM VIABLE REP:
If today is chaos, do the smallest honest version: spend five minutes creating the input and five minutes reviewing the output. Do not aim for a masterpiece. Aim for a usable artefact and one clear improvement. Keeping the chain alive matters because skill comes from repeated contact with real work, not from admiring the concept from a safe distance.

---------------
Use only material you are allowed to share with your chosen LLM. Anonymise anything sensitive. Treat AI outputs as drafts, not authority. Verify factual claims before using them in public, financial, medical, legal, HR, or high-stakes contexts.
The AI Habit: https://harrysharman.com/projects/ai-habit/
